title: Clear Street Announces Voluntary SEC Deregistration
content:
NEW YORK, Feb.  23, 2026  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Clear Street Group Inc. (“Clear Street” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: CLRS) today announced that the Company’s Board of Directors (the “Board”) determined to voluntarily deregister its Class A common stock in order to terminate and suspend its reporting obligations under the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The Board made the decision to pursue deregistration of the Company’s Class A common stock upon its determination to postpone the Company’s initial public offering in light of current market conditions.

The Company filed a registration statement withdrawal request on February 19, 2026 requesting withdrawal of its registration statement on Form S-1 and intends to file a Form 25 (Notification of Removal from Listing) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the “SEC”) on or about March 2, 2026. Following approval of the Company’s withdrawal request, the Company intends to file a Form 15 with the SEC to indefinitely suspend its reporting obligations under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.

About Clear Street: Clear Street’s mission is to give every sophisticated investor access to every asset, in every market, through a unified platform built for speed, transparency and scale. We give our clients the technology, tools, and service once reserved for the largest institutions, rebuilt with modern infrastructure. Our single, cloud-native, end-to-end capital markets platform powers investor growth today and transforms how they interact with markets tomorrow.

Forward-Looking and Cautionary Statements
This press release contains “forward-looking statements” within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, including statements regarding the expecting timing of the Company's delisting and deregistration of the Company’s Class A common stock. Each of these forward-looking statements involves risks and uncertainties. Actual results may differ materially from those, express or implied, in these forward-looking statements. We do not undertake any duty to update any forward-looking statement except as required by law.

Investor Contact:
IR@clearstreet.io

Media Contact:
press@clearstreet.io

title: Clear Street slashes IPO size after investors resist valuation
content:
[stock market chart trading board]
jpgfactory

Clear Street Group (CLRS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLRS]) cut the number of shares it's offering [https://seekingalpha.com/filings/pdf/19139351]by more than half and lowered its expected price range by ~36% after investors pushed back on its previous IPO expectations that could have valued the entire company at ~$12B.

The brokerage would get gross proceeds of ~$354M from the offering of 13M shares if it prices at the high end of the revised expected price range of $26-$28 per share. That's down from up to $1.05B that it might have received if the 23.8M shares it had expected to sell were priced at the high end of $40-$44 per share.

The slimmed-down IPO puts a total valuation of the company at $7.2B. Bloomberg News reported [https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/clear-street-said-to-weigh-downsizing-ipo-on-investor-pushback?srnd=homepage-americas]the expectation of a reduced IPO late Wednesday.

Clear Street (CLRS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLRS]) calls itself a financial infrastructure technology company that has built a cloud-native, end-to-end capital markets platform powered by a single real-time ledger.

Its expected IPO date is Friday, Feb. 13, according to the Nasdaq IPO calendar.

MORE ON CLEAR STREET GROUP INC.

 	* Clear Street files for IPO, seeks to raise ~$1.05B [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4546985-clear-street-files-for-ipo-seeks-to-raise-105b]

title: Catalyst Watch: Crypto swings, FedEx investor event, and Super Bowl bets
content:
[Stock exchange building columns stock market finance]
alexsl

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - FEBRUARY 9

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - ApplLovin (APP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/APP]) and Bloom Energy (BE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BE]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include TechCreate Group (TCGL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TCGL]), Peakstone Realty Trust (PKST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PKST]), and Frontera Energy (FECCF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FECCF]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Yext Inc. (YEXT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YEXT]), Grindr (GRND [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GRND]), and PayPal (PYPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PYPL]). The pressure in the cryptocurrency market is expected to keep MicroStrategy (MSTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSTR]), Marathon Digital (MARA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MARA]), Riot Platforms (RIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RIO]), and Hut 8 (HUT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HUT]) more volatile than normal, with Bitcoin (BTC-USD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BTC-USD]) down more than 20% on a year-to-date basis. 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Apollo Asset Management (APO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/APO]), On Semiconductor (ON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ON]) and Loews (L [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/L]).
 	* IPO WATCH - AGI (AGBK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AGBK]), Arko Petroleum (APC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/APC]), and Clear Street Group (CLRS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLRS]) are expected to price their IPOs and begin to trade. The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Bullish (BLSH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLSH]), Nasus Pharma (NSRX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NSRX]), and Magnitude International (MAGH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAGH]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Apple (AAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL]), Las Vegas Sands (LVS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LVS]), IBM (IBM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IBM]), and Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]).
 	* ALL DAY - The spinoff of Becton, Dickinson and Company's (BDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BDX]) biosciences & diagnostic solutions business and merger with Water Corporation (WAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WAT]) will become effective after the close.
 	* ALL DAY - Sports betting stocks DraftKings (DKNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DKNG]) and FanDuel (FLUT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLUT]) will be on watch as analysts digest the impact of the Super Bowl results on Q1 hold rates. The House has seen disappointing results vs. the betting public in the last two Super Bowls.

TUESDAY - FEBRUARY 10

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Coca-Cola (KO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KO]), Gilead Sciences (GILD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GILD]), S&P Global (SPGI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPGI]), BP (BP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BP]), Marriott International (MAR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAR]), Cloudflare (NET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NET]), Lyft (LYFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LYFT]), and Ford Motor (F [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/F]). Options trading implies a double digit swing in share price for Astera Labs (ALAB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALAB]) and Robinhood Markets (HOOD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HOOD]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - PACCAR (PCAR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PCAR]) will hold an Analyst Day event with research analysts and institutional shareholders.
 	* ALL DAY - The three-day CoinDesk Consensus Hong Kong event will kick off.
 	* ALL DAY - The EIA will publish its Short-Term Energy Outlook.
 	* 8:00 A.M. Bank of America (BAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAC]) CEO Brian Moynihan will appear at the BofA Securities Financial Services Conference.
 	* 8:15 A.M. CorMedix (CRMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRMD]) will hold an Investor Day event in New York City. The company will share background and updates across its portfolio and pipeline and outline key growth drivers and upcoming milestones.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The January Retail Sales report is expected to be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
 	* 8:50 A.M. Wells Fargo & Company (WFC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WFC]) CFO Mike Santomassimo will present at the UBS Financial Services Conference.

WEDNESDAY - FEBRUARY 11

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]), McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]), T-Mobile US (TMUS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TMUS]), Shopify (SHOP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SHOP]), Applovin (APP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/APP]), Hilton Worldwide (HLT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HLT]), and Kraft Heinz (KHC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KHC]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Vertex (VRT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VRT]) and QuantumScape (QS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QS]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]) will hold an Investor Day event to lay out multiyear strategy and financial targets. Analysts note that the event has extra significance due to the upcoming separation of the freight business.
 	* ALL DAY - Alimentation Couche-Tard (ATD:CA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ATD:CA]) will issue its 2026 business strategy update.
 	* ALL DAY - New York Fashion Week will kick off. Ralph Lauren (RL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RL]), PVH's (PVH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PVH]) Calvin Klein, Coach (TPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TPR]), and Michael Kors (CPRI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CPRI]) are some brands holding events.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The delayed January jobs report will be released. Economists expect 50K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.4%
 	* 9:40 A.M. Incoming Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]) CFO Gonzalo Luchetti will present at the Bank of America Securities Financial Services Conference.
 	* POSTMARKET - McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]) will hold its earnings conference call. Analysts will be looking to see if any sales momentum is led by pricing or traffic gains. Updates on menu innovation will also be closely watched. Yum! Brands (YUM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YUM]) is the restaurant stock that correlates the tightest to McDonald's, while supplier Lamb Weston (LW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LW]) trades in tandem with McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]) 75% of the time after earnings.

THURSDAY - FEBRUARY 12

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Applied Materials (AMAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMAT]), Arista Networks (ANET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ANET]), Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BUD]), Coinbase Global (COIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COIN]), Expedia (EXPE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EXPE]), Wynn Resorts (WYNN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WYNN]), and Airbnb (ABNB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABNB]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Pinterest (PINS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PINS]), Twilio (TWLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TWLO]), and Rivian Automotive (RIVN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RIVN]) after they report.
 	* M&A WATCH - The Brighthouse Financial (BHF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BHF]) buyout offer from Aquarian, the Ryerson Holding (RYI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RYI])-Olympic Steel (ZEUS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZEUS]) merger, and IBM's (IBM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IBM]) acquisition of Confluent (CFLT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CFLT]) all go to a shareholder vote.
 	* ALL DAY - Datadog (DDOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DDOG]) will host an Investor Day in New York City.
 	* ALL DAY - Federal Reserve Governor Lorie Logan and Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran will both speak at a Dallas Fed event.
 	* ALL DAY - The IEA will publish its monthly Oil Market Report.

FRIDAY - FEBRUARY 13

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Enbridge (ENB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ENB]) and Moderna (MRNA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRNA]).
 	* ALL DAY - The New York Toy Fair will take place over the weekend. Leading brands such as Hasbro (HAS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAS]), Mattel (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]), Spin Master (TOY:CA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TOY:CA]), and Jakks Pacific (JAKK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JAKK]) will unveil their 2026 product lines and highlight new licensing partnerships tied to blockbuster entertainment releases.
 	* ALL DAY- It is the one-year anniversary of the SailPoint (SAIL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SAIL]) IPO.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The Consumer Price Index report for December will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to be 2.6%.
